title: Braised Pork Chops with Vegetables
description: Boneless pork chops seared until golden then braised low and slow with onion, carrots, celery, mushrooms, potatoes, and tomatoes. Fork-tender, deeply flavored, one pot.

ingredients.name: cremini or baby bella mushrooms, sliced
cherry tomatoes, halved (or 1 cup diced tomatoes)
dry white wine or apple cider (optional)
frozen peas or green beans (optional, added at the end)

instructions.text: Pat pork chops dry and season all over with salt and pepper. Chop onion, carrots, and celery into 1-inch chunks. Mince garlic. Cube potatoes and hold in a bowl of cold water to prevent browning. Slice mushrooms.

Heat olive oil or butter in a heavy Dutch oven or enamel pan over medium heat. Sear chops 3–4 minutes per side until deep golden brown. Work in batches if needed — don't crowd the pan. Transfer to a plate.

Add onion, carrots, and celery to the same pan with a pinch of salt. Cook 5–6 minutes, stirring, until softened. Add mushrooms and cook until they release moisture and start to brown, about 5–7 minutes. Stir in garlic for 30–60 seconds.
Optional: splash in white wine or apple cider and simmer 1–2 minutes, scraping up the browned bits. Add broth, tomato paste, cherry tomatoes, thyme, and bay leaf if using. Stir to combine.

Drain potatoes and add to the pot. Nestle pork chops back in — liquid should come about halfway up the chops; add a splash of broth if needed. Bring to a bare simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook gently for 25 minutes. Keep it at a lazy simmer, not a hard boil.

If using peas or green beans, stir them in now for the last 5 minutes. Transfer chops to a warm plate to rest. Taste the sauce and adjust salt and pepper. Optional: mix 1 tbsp cornstarch with 1 tbsp cold water and stir in to thicken; or reduce uncovered 5 minutes. A teaspoon of cider vinegar or lemon juice brightens the sauce.
Return chops to the pan for 30 seconds to rewarm. Plate chops and spoon vegetables and sauce over the top.

notes:
Thinner chops (under 1 inch): check doneness at 20 minutes and pull them early; let the vegetables finish another 5–10 minutes without the chops.

Flavor swaps: rosemary instead of thyme; apple cider plus a sliced apple for an autumn version.

For a richer sauce: stir in 1 tbsp butter off heat just before serving for sheen and body.
